results.rationale: The mutation stem and prompt are complete, and the one-mark DNA-change evidence and model answer fit the line response.
The natural-selection prompt is self-contained; the any-three marking points and model answer fit the six-line response.
All four scientist options are visible, and the selectable key correctly identifies Alfred Wallace and Charles Darwin.
Inspected Figure 1 clearly labels the hoverfly and wasp, while the visible stem supplies the sting and shared-habitat facts needed by the three-mark mimicry explanation.
The assembled text states layer B's low pH, very low oxygen and dead plant remains, and inspected Figure 2 supplies the layer diagram; both accepted decay routes are gradeable.
The 340 g/m²/year value and all four choices are visible, and the keyed 10% transfer answer of 34 g/m²/year is selectable.
The phosphate context and four choices are complete, and DNA is both selectable and correctly keyed.
The nitrogen-compound context and four choices are complete, and Protein is both selectable and correctly keyed.
The visible text supplies peat uses and stored carbon dioxide/methane, and inspected Figures 2 and 3 render correctly; the four linked greenhouse-effect points fit the response.
All five sampling-method steps are visible, so an improvement can be proposed and graded against the broad accepted list and model answer.
The rendered Table 1 includes the 3-week values 9, 7, 10, X and total 32, supporting the correctly keyed result X = 6.
Table 1 supplies all six plotting pairs, and inspected Figure 4 is the complete blank graph response surface with its y-axis and grid; the plotting checklist matches the task.
The prior plotting task, Table 1 and inspected Figure 4 are visible, and the scheme explicitly grades a learner's valid graph readings while also providing the no-line 40% fallback.
The pond and timing scenario is visible, two labelled fields are provided, and the scheme offers a suitable range of independently gradeable causes.
Inspected Figure 6 clearly shows the stationary and rotating apparatus, and the dark-cupboard fact supports the correctly targeted phototropism control explanation.
Figure 6 visibly shows the damp blotting paper, the control permits two selections, and both correct reasons are present among the five options and in the key.
The visible apparatus comparison shows A stationary and B rotating slowly, supporting both control marks and the matching model answer.
Inspected Figures 5 and 6 supply the initial ink marks and orientation, and Figure 7 is a complete drawable response surface; all three drawing marks fit it.
The prior Figure 7 task and inspected Figure 6 remain visible, so the rotating-apparatus root can be compared with A and graded as straight or horizontal.
The apparatus orientation and prior root-result context are visible in inspected Figures 6 and 7, and the auxin-distribution and differential-growth points directly grade the explanation.
The prompt is self-contained, two labelled response fields render, and the accepted-use list plus model answer can grade two distinct auxin uses.
The gibberellin-on-apple-tree prompt is complete, two labelled fields render, and the accepted reasons and model answer fit the requested two suggestions.
Inspected Figure 8 clearly shows the ruler-drop method and 18 cm example, while two labelled fields and the accepted-improvements list fit the task.
The nervous-versus-endocrine comparison is self-contained, and the two labelled fields, comparative marking points and model answer align.
Inspected Figure 9 clearly points A to the thyroid gland, and thyroxine is the correct keyed and model response.
Inspected Figure 9 clearly points B to the pancreas, and the scheme and model accept an appropriate pancreatic hormone.
The adrenaline prompt is self-contained, two labelled fields render, and the broad accepted-effects evidence can grade two valid effects.
Both contraception and infertility are explicitly requested, a suitable extended response renders, and the level descriptors, indicative content, checklist and model answer cover both halves.
The dominance prompt is complete, and the heterozygous-expression mark and model answer fit the short line response.
Inspected Figure 10 clearly shows affected person 1 and unaffected child 5, and the D/d key is visible, supporting the two heterozygosity marks.
Figure 10 and the allele symbols supply both parental phenotypes, the blank drawing box can render a Punnett square, and the four derivation marks and model answer fit.
The recessive-disorder frequency problem includes the required 1-in-50 value, and the four linked inheritance/probability points correctly establish 1 in 2500.
All production, energy, efficiency and conversion values are visible, and the staged scheme and model correctly lead to 2190 m² to three significant figures.
The maize mass and chicken count/mean mass are visible, and inspected Figure 11 is the complete centred blank grid response surface required by the biomass-pyramid scheme.
The prior 4200 kg and 1000 × 2.2 kg data are restated, the paired ratio blanks render, and their canonical key 11:21 matches the simplest-form requirement and scheme.
The biomass-use percentages and requested remainder are explicit, and the egestion/faeces marking evidence and model answer fit the line response.
Rendered Table 2 contains all seven normal-maize and QPM values, and the visible option Lysine and tryptophan is correctly keyed.
The essential-amino-acid explanation and Table 2 leucine values are visible, and the accepted sufficiency explanations and model answer directly grade the suggestion.
The homeostasis prompt is self-contained, and the two definition components and model answer fit the four-line response.
The prompt clearly names glucose, protein and urea, and the filtration, reabsorption and urine points plus model answer cover all requested substances.
The ADH prompt is complete, and the four linked permeability, reabsorption, concentration and volume points align with the model answer and response space.
The visible stem explains glyphosate resistance and field spraying, and the level scheme, corrected soya-specific checklist and model answer support a full linked yield explanation.
The GM/glyphosate context and exclusions are explicit, two labelled fields render, and the accepted concern list and model answer can grade two non-ethical reasons.

rationale: All 43 learner-visible contexts contain the facts, structured tables, prior subparts and response surfaces needed to answer. All 11 referenced local figures were inspected and are complete; response controls are renderable, answer keys align with the visible questions, and written or drawing responses have usable model answers and mark-scheme evidence.
